candidates.title: Layer-Dependent Absorption and Inverted-U Pattern
candidates.summary: Absorption is highly layer-dependent: 49.3% at layer 4 (CONFIRMED) but only 0.19% at layer 8 (FALSIFIED). The inverted-U pattern peaks at mid-layers. Primary contribution: first systematic documentation of layer-dependent absorption with implications for SAE reliability at different model depths. This iteration addresses supervisor review (score 6.0, stagnant 9 iterations) critical issues: H4 causal conclusion removed, H6 elevated to critical path.
hypotheses: H2 (token frequency correlation - PENDING, critical path, MUST run at layer 4 with 12,000 latents)
H6 (perfect-score latents - PENDING, critical path, likely positional artifacts, confirmed at ALL dict sizes)
candidates.pilot_focus: H2 Spearman correlation at layer 4 (12,000 latents) + H6 perfect-score latent positional investigation (~4 hours CPU total, no GPU needed). STAGNATION: H2+H6 deferred 11 iterations.
candidates.novelty: First systematic documentation of layer-dependent absorption patterns in SAEs; RVE-based Af metric with random dictionary controls; dictionary size scaling law
candidates.supervisor_review: Score 6.0, stagnant for 9 consecutive iterations. Critical issues: (1) H4 causal conclusion removed per supervisor; (2) H6 investigation elevated to critical path per supervisor; (3) writing quality no longer the bottleneck - experimental execution is.
risks: H1 at layer 8 is strongly falsified
H2 pivotal: no correlation found = weakened mechanistic story
H6 perfect-score latents need investigation (confirmed at all dict sizes)
Mechanistic explanation for inverted-U is post-hoc
STAGNATION: H2+H6 pending 11 iterations without execution
CRITICAL: H2 was run at wrong layer (8 vs 4)
H5 limitation: subsets not independent dictionaries
next_steps: H2 analysis at layer 4 (12,000 absorbed latents) - CRITICAL, must redo at correct layer
H6 positional artifact investigation (~2 hours CPU) - CRITICAL, confirmed at all dict sizes
Multi-seed confirmation of layer-dependence (if H2 positive)
Test on GemmaScope SAEs for generalization (if H2 positive)

candidates.title: Absorption Metric with Validation Framework
candidates.summary: The absorption metric (Af) with random dictionary validation is the primary contribution. Establishes standard practice for SAE quality assessment. Pivot target if H2 is falsified.
hypotheses: Metric validation only (no new experiments required)
candidates.novelty: Af metric operationalization differs from prior work by using RVE-based absorption with random dictionary controls
next_steps: Apply metric to multiple SAE/model combinations

candidates.title: SAE Training Curriculum for Absorption Control
candidates.summary: Train SAEs with controlled L1 sparsity to find optimal lambda balancing sparsity against absorption. Directly addresses H3's L0/L1 conflation.
candidates.pilot_focus: Train mini-SAEs with L1=[4e-5, 8e-5, 1.6e-4] on 10M tokens (~6 GPU hours total)
candidates.novelty: First systematic study of absorption as function of training hyperparameters
risks: Still may not address H4 circuit faithfulness

candidates.title: GemmaScope Multi-Layer SAE Generalization
candidates.summary: Test whether layer-dependent absorption pattern generalizes across different SAE architectures (GemmaScope) and model sizes. Addresses GPT2-small specificity concern.
hypotheses: H1 layer-dependence replication on GemmaScope SAEs
candidates.pilot_focus: Run H1 analysis on GemmaScope SAE at layers 0/4/8 (~6 GPU hours)
candidates.novelty: First cross-architecture validation of layer-dependent absorption
risks: Does not address H4 circuit faithfulness
Pattern may not replicate on different architecture
next_steps: Obtain GemmaScope SAE activations at layers 0/4/8
Compare inverted-U pattern with GPT2-small results
